title: Photosynthesis
definition: Photosynthesis is the process by which plants, algae, and some bacteria convert light energy into chemical energy, using carbon dioxide and water to produce glucose and oxygen.

proofs.description: Van Niel's experiments establishing oxygen source from water
proofs.reference: Van Niel, C. B. (1931). Photosynthesis in bacteria

difficulty_levels.beginner: Plants use sunlight to make food from CO2 and water
difficulty_levels.intermediate: Light-dependent reactions produce ATP and NADPH for the Calvin cycle
difficulty_levels.expert: Photosystem II and I work in series to reduce NADP+ to NADPH
